steps.title: Fastest path: use the perimeter formulas
steps.content: Find the total perimeter from each type of shape separately. A square contributes 4 side lengths, and an equilateral triangle contributes 3 side lengths, so multiply each shape count by its number of sides.

steps.title: Find the total perimeter of the squares
steps.content: Each square has 4 sides of length $r$, so each square has perimeter $4r$. With 4 squares: $$4 \times 4r = 16r$$

steps.title: Find the total perimeter of the triangles
steps.content: Each equilateral triangle has 3 sides of length $t$, so each triangle has perimeter $3t$. With 5 triangles: $$5 \times 3t = 15t$$

steps.title: Write the total and eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
The combined perimeter is $16r + 15t = 260$.
  • A: $12r + 20t = 260$ — Wrong coefficients; 4 squares give $16r$, not $12r$. Eliminated.
  • B: $4r + 5t = 260$ — Uses counts instead of perimeters (ignores side counts). Eliminated.
  • C: $16r + 15t = 260$ — $4 \times 4r = 16r$ and $5 \times 3t = 15t$. That is the correct answer.
  • D: $5r + 4t = 260$ — Swaps the shape counts and ignores side counts. Eliminated.
